units.title: Remediate src/game (4 findings)

findings.title: CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD
findings.detail: compared at 1 site(s) (e.g. healthPercentage <= CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD); no test names CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD, so the boundary case (== CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD) is likely untested

extra.title: CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD is compared as a limit but no test references it
extra.subject: CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD
extra.detail: compared at 1 site(s) (e.g. healthPercentage <= CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD); no test names CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD, so the boundary case (== CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD) is likely untested

evidence: detail
compared at 1 site(s) (e.g. healthPercentage <= CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD); no test names CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD, so the boundary case (== CRITICAL_HEALTH_THRESHOLD) is likely untested

findings.title: silent failure: a corrupt stored config is caught and discarded to console.error
findings.detail: Real but low blast radius: a JSON.parse failure on localStorage logs to the console and falls back to defaults, dropping the user's saved settings with no in-game signal.

extra.swallowKind: a corrupt stored config is caught and discarded to console.error
extra.realVsBenign: Real but low blast radius: a JSON.parse failure on localStorage logs to the console and falls back to defaults, dropping the user's saved settings with no in-game signal.

evidence: swallows
a corrupt stored config is caught and discarded to console.error
real vs benign
Real but low blast radius: a JSON.parse failure on localStorage logs to the console and falls back to defaults, dropping the user's saved settings with no in-game signal.

findings.title: footgun: removeSystem(system) runs `system.destroy;`, reading as a destroy() call
findings.detail: It is a bare property access with no parentheses, so destroy() never runs. clearSystems() at line 226 calls system.destroy() correctly, so a system removed via removeSystem keeps its listeners and timers; the two teardown paths disagree.

extra.appearance: removeSystem(system) runs `system.destroy;`, reading as a destroy() call
extra.reality: It is a bare property access with no parentheses, so destroy() never runs. clearSystems() at line 226 calls system.destroy() correctly, so a system removed via removeSystem keeps its listeners and timers; the two teardown paths disagree.

evidence: appears
removeSystem(system) runs `system.destroy;`, reading as a destroy() call
actually

It is a bare property access with no parentheses, so destroy() never runs. clearSystems() at line 226 calls system.destroy() correctly, so a system removed via removeSystem keeps its listeners and timers; the two teardown paths disagree.

units.summary: ECS core: the Entity-Component-System framework that registers entities/components/systems and defers entity destruction to end of frame.

units.title: Remediate src/tests (2 findings)

findings.title: silent failure: a requested test that does not exist logs an error but never exits non-zero
findings.detail: Real: the runner prints the not-found message and falls through to exit 0, so CI reads success when the named test never ran.

extra.swallowKind: a requested test that does not exist logs an error but never exits non-zero
extra.realVsBenign: Real: the runner prints the not-found message and falls through to exit 0, so CI reads success when the named test never ran.

evidence: swallows
a requested test that does not exist logs an error but never exits non-zero
real vs benign
Real: the runner prints the not-found message and falls through to exit 0, so CI reads success when the named test never ran.

findings.title: silent failure: an empty test set runs zero tests and still exits 0
findings.detail: Real: when allTests is empty the forEach does nothing and the process exits success, so the suite reads as passing when nothing was exercised.

extra.swallowKind: an empty test set runs zero tests and still exits 0
extra.realVsBenign: Real: when allTests is empty the forEach does nothing and the process exits success, so the suite reads as passing when nothing was exercised.

evidence: swallows
an empty test set runs zero tests and still exits 0
real vs benign
Real: when allTests is empty the forEach does nothing and the process exits success, so the suite reads as passing when nothing was exercised.

units.summary: Custom test runner: a ts-node script that discovers and runs .test.ts files from the cases directory with colored pass/fail output.

units.title: Remediate src/ui (1 finding)

findings.title: The nested onComplete timer is untracked by the effect cleanup
findings.detail: The useEffect cleanup clears only the outer setTimeout (line 47); the inner setTimeout(onComplete, 400) at line 44 is not tracked, so if the component unmounts during the 400ms fade-out, onComplete fires after unmount as a stale state update.

extra.consequence: The useEffect cleanup clears only the outer setTimeout (line 47); the inner setTimeout(onComplete, 400) at line 44 is not tracked, so if the component unmounts during the 400ms fade-out, onComplete fires after unmount as a stale state update.

evidence: consequence

The useEffect cleanup clears only the outer setTimeout (line 47); the inner setTimeout(onComplete, 400) at line 44 is not tracked, so if the component unmounts during the 400ms fade-out, onComplete fires after unmount as a stale state update.

units.summary: UI game-state hooks: custom hooks that subscribe to EventBus events and feed React the live game state (coins, health, XP, camera, level, player attributes, abilities, upgrades, spawn points).

units.title: Remediate src/utils (1 finding)

findings.title: normalizeValue divides by zero when min equals max
findings.detail: (value - min) / (max - min) returns NaN when min === max, with no guard, so a degenerate range silently propagates NaN through scaling and interpolation that depend on it.

extra.consequence: (value - min) / (max - min) returns NaN when min === max, with no guard, so a degenerate range silently propagates NaN through scaling and interpolation that depend on it.

evidence: consequence
(value - min) / (max - min) returns NaN when min === max, with no guard, so a degenerate range silently propagates NaN through scaling and interpolation that depend on it.

units.summary: Math utilities: vector/position math (clamp, distance, normalize, lerp), weighted random selection, noise remap and a bell-curve distribution.
